page: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, VNNI, and BF16, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. This hardware profile delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by stress-ng and Passmark CPU benchmarks, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth for cached small blocks.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core and top-tier multi-core performance. It achieves top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, processing 122.32 million operations per second. For large language model inference, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds with medium-sized models and strong performance with large models, though single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average tier.

The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear architectural tradeoff, positioning this instance as a cost-efficient option for pure CPU-bound workloads. The high compute density and DDR5 memory speed make it highly suitable for workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing, alongside strong database and Redis SET operations. It is highly efficient for CPU-bound tasks, including software compilation, physics simulations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and DDR5 memory speeds for intensive CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Optimized for CPU-bound workloads, database operations, and LLM inference
